programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: Apple announces the iPhone; Mike scrambles to please Verizon; Jim is unreachable trying to make a personal business deal; the Securities and Exchange Commission investigates the company.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank rants against Railton College, calling it "Mediocrity's Capitol," and the administration is pressured to fire him; Hank and Lily contemplate a future outside of Railton.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: When George Saunders visits Railton College, the professors scramble to impress the celebrated writer, while Hank is reminded of his own failed writing career; Hank and Lily anticipate an announcement from their daughter Julie.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank spirals when a storage pod full of his father's belongings is dropped in his front yard; the professors spiral when rumors of budget cuts threaten their tenured jobs.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Amid firing rumors, the professors look to the union to protect their jobs, while Hank tries a different approach; Lily interviews for a job at a prestigious New York prep school.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank and Lily host the annual English department dinner; Lily has a decision to make; Hank's past involving his troubled relationship with his father catches up to him.

programme.title: Halt and Catch Fire
programme.desc: Cameron promotes her game at an industry conference; Donna squares off against old rivals; Gordon struggles to support Joe's newest idea.

programme.title: Halt and Catch Fire
programme.desc: Cameron buys a plot of land to recenter; Joe and Gordon hire a team of websurfers to map the web; Donna focuses her team on the mission ahead.

programme.title: Halt and Catch Fire
programme.desc: Donna struggles with suspicion; Bosworth hits a breaking point; Joe confesses to Gordon while Cameron faces past mistakes.

programme.title: Halt and Catch Fire
programme.desc: Donna makes a play for a heavy-hitter; Gordon confronts his daughter about an issue at school; Cameron finds a new fan; Joe sees a new side of Haley.

programme.title: Halt and Catch Fire
programme.desc: Joe and Gordon discuss Comet's future; Donna takes a vacation; a new collaborator challenges Cameron; Bos and Diane make a life-altering choice.

programme.title: Halt and Catch Fire
programme.desc: Cameron helps Donna pack up; Bos makes his famous chili; Joe helps Haley recover a memorable keepsake; Gordon and Donna fight.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: A rival ad agency uses Betty to try to lure Don away from Sterling Cooper; the agency tries to counter the latest Kennedy ads.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don loses an important account so Roger attempts to cheer him up with the aid of twin models; Joan's roommate makes an unnerving confession.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Peggy deals with a difficult assignment; Don finds new opportunities in Roger's problems; Betty finds an outlet for her growing dissatisfaction.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: The staff at Sterling Cooper stay up all night to watch the Nixon/Kennedy election returns; Pete Campbell's ambitions cause him to directly challenge Don.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Work responsibilities interfere with Don's domestic life; Peggy is given another opportunity; Betty makes a discovery.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Sterling Cooper advertising agency receives some new office equipment; Don and Duck are at odds over an account; Betty reconnects with an old friend.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Paul has a party at his apartment; Peggy visits her family; the agency pursues an account despite a conflict in interest; Duck uses an unorthodox approach with a client.

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: Mike Lazaridis and Doug Fregin pitch their idea of combining a computer with a cellphone to Jim Balsillie; Jim comes on board, and they try to sell their idea.

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: BlackBerry hits the market; Mike and Jim try to avoid a hostile takeover; Jim strategizes to increase sales.

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: Apple announces the iPhone; Mike scrambles to please Verizon; Jim is unreachable trying to make a personal business deal; the Securities and Exchange Commission investigates the company.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Disenchanted investigative journalist Martin Scarsden is told by his boss to write a follow-up piece a year after a mass shooting of five parishioners by a charismatic young priest in the small rural town of Riversend.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin Scarsden decides to stay on in Riversend to dig deeper into the mystery of Byron Swift, a well-liked priest who suddenly murdered five of his parishioners.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: At teenager Allen's wake, Martin learns that the boy was not as innocent as first portrayed, with Robbie telling him he was persuaded by Byron to drop drug charges against him.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin believes he has found the link between the shooting victims and asks for Robbie's help in accessing the case files; a further clue implicates one of the victims.

programme.title: The Audacity
programme.desc: In a world of jaded billionaires, psychiatrist-gurus, bio-hacked tech bros, AI labs and disillusioned teens being optimized in elite private schools, an audacious data-mining CEO strives to turn insight and influence into profit and power.
